data.title: Stock market falls ahead of Mag 7 earnings, Fed rate decision
data.description:
Wall Street opened in the red on Wednesday as traders looked forward to earnings from some Magnificent 7 companies and the Federal Reserve’s rate decision.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.3%. 

Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN) will release their results after the bell. 

The Federal Open Market Committee will decide on monetary policy, with rates expected to remain unchanged. The central bank decision is being made as Fed Chair Jerome Powell helms his last meeting, with Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh waiting in the wings.

Durable goods rebounded more than expected in March. Housing starts rose in March, while building permits fell.

The U.S. international trade in goods deficit widened in March. The wholesale and retail inventories accelerated M/M in March.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1.2 basis points to 4.37%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up 0.9 basis points to 3.86%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) added 1.2 basis points to 4.95%.

data.title: Capitol Federal Financial GAAP EPS of $0.16 in-line, revenue of $57.7M beats by $0.73M
data.description:
• Capitol Federal Financial press release (CFFN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.16 in-line.
  • Revenue of $57.7M beats by $0.73M.
  • an efficiency ratio of 52.45%, an improvement from 53.66% the prior quarter;
  • an operating expense ratio of 1.24%, unchanged from the prior quarter;
  • paid dividends of $15.9 million, or $0.125 per share, including a $0.040 per share special dividend; and
  • repurchased 2,155,481 shares of common stock at an average price of $7.16 per share.

data.title: Graphic Packaging, NextEra Energy to build 250 MW solar plant in Texas
data.description:
Graphic Packaging (GPK) said Wednesday it has signed an agreement with NextEra Energy (NEE) to build a 250 MW solar power plant in west Texas, aiming to ‌source renewable electricity and reduce its greenhouse gas emissions.

Graphic Packaging (GPK) said the solar plant, Selenite Springs Energy Center, would be ​located in the ERCOT power market and is expected to start commercial ‌operations ⁠by year-end 2027.

The company said it will be the sole buyer of the facility's renewable energy attribute certificates, expected to cover 43% of its 2025 electricity usage in the U.S. and Canada.

The deal is Graphic Packaging's (GPK) largest virtual power purchase agreement to date and forms part of its target to cut global Scope 1 and 2 ​emissions by 50.4% ​by 2032, adding that the deal would increase its total ​global purchased renewable electricity to ~49%.

data.title: Vita Coco shares eye record high on growing demand for coconut water
data.description:
Shares of Vita Coco (COCO) are rallying into Wednesday’s open and are expected to set an all-time high during regular trade as the growing popularity of coconut water fueled double-digit revenue growth and a 300 basis point expansion in the company’s profit margin.

Moreover, the strong first quarter results encouraged the company to set ambitious goals for FY26, raising its sales outlook by 5.4% from the prior forecast and increasing its adjusted EBITDA guidance by $10M, reflecting continued brand strength and improving private label shipment trends.

“The coconut water category continues to be one of the fastest-growing beverage categories in both the United States and our core international markets, which we believe is due to consumers choosing coconut water for more of their hydration needs,” Vita Coco CEO Michael Kirban said. “I believe this growth is being largely driven by our investments as the category leader, resulting in increased household penetration and new consumption occasions for coconut water and for the Vita Coco brand.”

Net sales for the first quarter swelled 37% to a better-than-expected $49M, driven by case equivalent volume gains of 32% for Vita Coco Coconut Water and +27% for the private label sales, along with higher net pricing.

This contributed to a 50% increase in gross profit despite higher finished goods, tariff-related costs, and higher logistics expenses. On a per-share basis, the company earned a profit of $0.50 per share, up 61% year-over-year and $0.18 better than expected. Additionally, non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA increased by $16M.

For FY26, the company now expects sales to be between $720M and $725M compared to the $696.8M consensus estimate. Gross margin is seen at ~38%, unchanged from the prior outlook, as lower tariff costs and higher pricing are expected to be offset by an adverse product mix, inflationary impacts, and increased promotion and incentive costs.

data.title: BioHarvest appoints Zaki Rakib as CEO
data.description:
• BioHarvest Sciences (BHST) announced on Wednesday a leadership transition that consolidates operational control of the company’s contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) under Dr. Zaki Rakib, who has assumed the role of chief executive officer. 
  • Ilan Sobel, who had served as CEO since 2020, has left the executive role and joined the company’s Board of Directors with a focused mandate on advancing the direct-to-consumer (D2C) business.

data.title: XTL Biopharmaceuticals to acquire Psyga Bio in all-stock deal; shares surge
data.description:
• XTL Biopharmaceuticals (XTLB) agreed to acquire 100% of Psyga Bio in a share-based transaction.
  • Psyga shareholders will receive shares representing 40% of XTL post-closing, with no cash consideration.
  • Additional 10% equity may be issued upon achieving key clinical and commercialization milestones.
  • Psyga brings a GMP-ready facility and a library of 180+ proprietary mushroom strains.
  • The company has seven Phase 2a clinical trials planned across mental health and CNS indications.
  • XTL also secured a $1.5M private placement to support the transaction.
  • Psyga will operate as a wholly owned subsidiary following deal completion.
  • XTLB shares up 58.7% premarket.

data.title: O'Keefe Stevens Advisory adds BAX; exits TPH and BABA
data.description:
• O’Keefe Stevens Advisory underperformed a broadly weaker market in Q1 2026, with the S&P 500 declining 4.3% and the Nasdaq Composite falling 7.0% over the quarter.
  • The portfolio initiated a new position in Baxter International (NYSE: BAX) in Q1 2026.
  • Among sales, the strategy exited Tri Pointe Homes (NYSE: TPH) and Alibaba (NYSE: BABA) in Q1 2026.
  • Source.
